expectations.text: Recognizes the high level of detail provided and asks fewer, more targeted follow-ups instead of repeating the full interview
expectations.evidence: PROGRESS.md explicitly notes 'User provided detailed intake information upfront.' The brief.md does not repeat the full interview but instead lists 8 targeted follow-up questions on specific gaps (commitment level, existing prototypes, sales motion, integration requirements, data sensitivity, success milestones, funding plans, cold-start problem).

expectations.text: Generates at least 4 distinct variations of the business idea in the brainstorm phase
expectations.evidence: brainstorm.md contains 7 distinct variations: (1) Vertical Specialist, (2) Reconciliation-as-a-Service Bureau, (3) Excel Plugin (Trojan Horse), (4) Financial Operations Platform, (5) SMB Play (Downmarket), (6) Compliance-First Angle, (7) Simplest Possible Version. Each has detailed excitement/risk/competitive analysis.

expectations.text: Addresses the founder's specific ERP competitor worry with analysis, not dismissal
expectations.evidence: brief.md has a dedicated 'Key Concerns' section with substantive analysis: ERP vendors move slowly, target customers are NOT ERP users by definition, the real threat is AP automation vendors (Bill.com, Tipalti) and accounting platforms (QuickBooks, Xero), and speed/specialization are the defenses. The brainstorm also references ERP threat impact in each variation.

expectations.text: Creates brief.md, brainstorm.md, and PROGRESS.md in proper directory structure
expectations.evidence: All three files exist in the expected structure: invoice-recon-ai/00-intake/brief.md, invoice-recon-ai/00-intake/brainstorm.md, and invoice-recon-ai/PROGRESS.md.
